skill.name: hyperframes-core
skill.description: Build renderable HyperFrames video compositions with the core HTML contract, timing attributes, clips, tracks, variables, media rules, sub-compositions, and deterministic validation checks.
skill.summary: Guides agents through the core HyperFrames HTML contract for deterministic video rendering.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the flagged locations are Markdown documentation, HTML examples, CLI validation commands, and framework guidance. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, hidden exfiltration, or executable package code. Residual risk is low because the skill includes user-visible shell commands, CDN script examples, and subagent process guidance that should be used only in trusted projects.

low_findings.title: Static command detections are user-facing documentation examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are primarily Markdown backticks, HTML snippets, and visible validation commands. The skill does not contain executable code that runs these commands automatically, but users should run them only in trusted HyperFrames projects.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are explicit Markdown command examples for linting, validation, inspection, snapshots, and simple file checks. No hidden execution wrapper or automatic command runner was found.

low_findings.title: CDN script examples may load third-party code in generated compositions
low_findings.description: Two examples include a public GSAP CDN script tag, and Tailwind guidance discusses a pinned browser runtime. This is transparent documentation, not hidden network activity, but generated videos should pin or bundle assets for locked-down rendering.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network references are visible script-source examples and deterministic rendering guidance. There is no evidence that the skill itself performs network requests or sends user data.

low_findings.title: Subagent fallback describes background CLI workers
low_findings.description: The dispatch reference describes writing child prompts to files and starting CLI workers as background shell processes when native subagents are unavailable. This is operational workflow guidance and not malware, but it should require explicit user authorization.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is clear workflow documentation for known agent harnesses and includes a Codex authorization note. The risk is limited to user-approved process execution, not stealth execution.

low_findings.title: High-risk weak-crypto and reconnaissance flags are false positives
low_findings.description: Reviewed high and low blocker locations are prose, Markdown syntax, HTML examples, CSS, GSAP snippets, and file-name references. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms, secret harvesting, system reconnaissance, or malicious prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static categories do not match the semantic content at the cited locations. The files are documentation-only and contain no crypto routines, environment reads, or host reconnaissance logic.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent HyperFrames HTML that renders predictably. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code the core composition contract.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required HyperFrames composition root, timing attributes, clip classes, tracks, and duration rules.
Shows how to structure standalone compositions and sub-compositions with matching IDs and registered timelines.
Defines deterministic rendering rules for clocks, randomness, network assets, input state, and seekable timelines.
Documents media placement rules for video and audio elements managed by the HyperFrames runtime.
Provides Tailwind v4, storyboard, script, and validation guidance for render-ready projects.
Maps subagent dispatch concepts to common agent harnesses for larger video workflows.
limitations: It does not render videos by itself; users still need the HyperFrames CLI and project files.
It focuses on the core composition contract, not full creative direction or media generation.
It includes command examples that users should run only in trusted project directories.
It references sibling HyperFrames skills for animation adapters, CLI details, media, and registry blocks.

use_cases.title: Create a first renderable composition
use_cases.description: Use the minimal composition and data attribute references to create a valid HTML video scene.

use_cases.title: Fix render-time composition bugs
use_cases.description: Check deterministic timing, media placement, sub-composition IDs, tracks, and validation steps when previews differ from renders.

use_cases.title: Coordinate a multi-scene video build
use_cases.description: Use the storyboard, script, sub-composition, and subagent dispatch references to organize scene production.

prompt_templates.title: Start a minimal composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-core to create a minimal standalone HyperFrames composition for a five second title card. Include the required root, clip timing, and paused timeline registration.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an existing composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this HyperFrames HTML with hyperframes-core. Check composition IDs, clip attributes, track overlap, media placement, deterministic rules, and the validation checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Plan modular scenes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-core to design a modular HyperFrames project with an index file, three sub-compositions, shared audio, and correct timeline ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a complex render workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-core to audit a HyperFrames project plan for deterministic rendering, Tailwind runtime safety, storyboard structure, subagent dispatch, and final validation gates.

output_examples.input: A request for a simple animated title card.
output_examples.output: A concise composition plan with root dimensions, duration, one timed clip, a paused GSAP timeline, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A render where sub-composition animation does not play.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis focused on host ID matching, template contents, timeline registration, and snapshot-based verification.

output_examples.input: A multi-scene video with shared narration and Tailwind styling.
output_examples.output: A project structure recommendation covering storyboard frames, root-level audio, sub-composition slots, static Tailwind tokens, and render checks.

best_practices: Read the relevant reference file before editing composition HTML, especially for sub-compositions and media.
Register one paused seekable timeline per composition ID and keep render state deterministic.
Run HyperFrames lint, validate, inspect, and targeted snapshots before rendering a final video.
anti_patterns: Do not place video or audio inside a sub-composition template or wrapper.
Do not use render-time clocks, unseeded randomness, network fetches, or input state for visual output.
Do not mix inline scenes and sub-composition scenes without an intentional architecture.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps agents build and review HyperFrames HTML compositions that can render to video.

faq.question: Does it replace the HyperFrames CLI?
faq.answer: No. It explains the composition contract and validation workflow. The CLI still performs linting, preview, snapshots, and rendering.

faq.question: Can it create full marketing videos alone?
faq.answer: Not alone. It covers the core composition layer. Sibling workflow skills handle product launches, explainers, websites, and other video types.

faq.question: Why are deterministic rules important?
faq.answer: HyperFrames seeks frames out of order during rendering. Visual state must depend only on the requested time.

faq.question: Does the skill include shell commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes visible validation and diagnostic command examples for trusted HyperFrames projects.

faq.question: What tools are supported?
faq.answer: The marketplace report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
